revision.comment: Auto generating page. date placed far in the past so it is attributed to Immanuelle and FireflyBot sends them messages if it is ever imported to Wikipedia.
1 revision imported: importing redirects I created with chatGPT

revision.text: {{documentation subpage}} {{Ombox|type=content|text=Instead of this template, consider using the data-sort-value attribute, like so: | data-sort-value="Doe, John" | John Doe. Please see {{Section link|WP:SORT|Specifying a sort key for a cell}} for details.}} {{#ifeq:{{SUBPAGENAME}}|sandbox||{{High-use}}}} {{lua|Module:Sort}} This template is for manually adjusting sorting order in sortable tables by specified sortkeys. ; Usage: : {{Sort | 1 = {{{sort value}}} | 2 = {{{displayed value}}} }} → {{sort | 1 = {{{sort value}}} | 2 = {{{displayed value}}} }} The first parameter is the sortkey; the second parameter is the wikitext of what has to be displayed. By default, this is the sortkey with link brackets: : {{Sort | 1 = {{{displayed value}}} }} → {{sort | 1 = {{{displayed value}}} }} A table using this template should use it in all rows. This template works by adding a hidden sort value, which replaces the value of the visible text for sorting purposes. The HTML output looks like: [[displayed value]]. =====Example===== |{{Sort|Brothers Karamazov|[[The Brothers Karamazov]]}} → |{{Sort|Brothers Karamazov|[[The Brothers Karamazov]]|demo=yes}} ;Sorting by numerical value, date, etc. :See [[Help:Sortable tables#Numerical sorting problems]] and [[meta:Help:Sorting#Sort modes]] ;Equal rank :If you simply code as the second parameter an indicator that two items are equally ranked, e.g. "4=", the template interpreter will treat this as an additional parameter (i.e. parameter 4, which it will then not use). You must explicitly code this as parameter 2: {{Sort|4|2=4=}} ==See also== * {{tiw|Dsv}} – a shortcut for data-sort-value="''foo''" * {{tiw|Sortname}} — for using names in sortable tables (using "last name, first name" as default sort key) * {{tiw|Date table sorting}} — for using dates in sortable tables * {{tiw|Number table sorting}} — for using alphabetic sort mode for numbers in sortable tables * [[Help:Sortable tables]] * [[meta:Help:Sorting]] {{Sandbox other|| [[Category:Sorting templates|{{PAGENAME}}]] }}

revision.comment: Bot: add {{wikidata link|Q...}} or [[Category:Templates without wikidata]]
revision.text: {{documentation subpage}} {{Ombox|type=content|text=Instead of this template, consider using the data-sort-value attribute, like so: | data-sort-value="Doe, John" | John Doe. Please see {{Section link|WP:SORT|Specifying a sort key for a cell}} for details.}} {{#ifeq:{{SUBPAGENAME}}|sandbox||{{High-use}}}} {{lua|Module:Sort}} This template is for manually adjusting sorting order in sortable tables by specified sortkeys. ; Usage: : {{Sort | 1 = {{{sort value}}} | 2 = {{{displayed value}}} }} → {{sort | 1 = {{{sort value}}} | 2 = {{{displayed value}}} }} The first parameter is the sortkey; the second parameter is the wikitext of what has to be displayed. By default, this is the sortkey with link brackets: : {{Sort | 1 = {{{displayed value}}} }} → {{sort | 1 = {{{displayed value}}} }} A table using this template should use it in all rows. This template works by adding a hidden sort value, which replaces the value of the visible text for sorting purposes. The HTML output looks like: [[displayed value]]. =====Example===== |{{Sort|Brothers Karamazov|[[The Brothers Karamazov]]}} → |{{Sort|Brothers Karamazov|[[The Brothers Karamazov]]|demo=yes}} ;Sorting by numerical value, date, etc. :See [[Help:Sortable tables#Numerical sorting problems]] and [[meta:Help:Sorting#Sort modes]] ;Equal rank :If you simply code as the second parameter an indicator that two items are equally ranked, e.g. "4=", the template interpreter will treat this as an additional parameter (i.e. parameter 4, which it will then not use). You must explicitly code this as parameter 2: {{Sort|4|2=4=}} ==See also== * {{tiw|Dsv}} – a shortcut for data-sort-value="''foo''" * {{tiw|Sortname}} — for using names in sortable tables (using "last name, first name" as default sort key) * {{tiw|Date table sorting}} — for using dates in sortable tables * {{tiw|Number table sorting}} — for using alphabetic sort mode for numbers in sortable tables * [[Help:Sortable tables]] * [[meta:Help:Sorting]] {{Sandbox other|| [[Category:Sorting templates|{{PAGENAME}}]] }} [[Category:Templates without wikidata]]

revision.comment: imported only latest revision from https://archive.org/details/miraheze-wiki-shintowiki-20251218 to avoid excessive disk usage, comment on the revision was Text replacement - "↵<noinclude>[[Category:Templates without wikidata]]</noinclude>" to ""
revision.text: {{documentation subpage}} {{Ombox|type=content|text=Instead of this template, consider using the data-sort-value attribute, like so: | data-sort-value="Doe, John" | John Doe. Please see {{Section link|WP:SORT|Specifying a sort key for a cell}} for details.}} {{#ifeq:{{SUBPAGENAME}}|sandbox||{{High-use}}}} {{lua|Module:Sort}} This template is for manually adjusting sorting order in sortable tables by specified sortkeys. ; Usage: : {{Sort | 1 = {{{sort value}}} | 2 = {{{displayed value}}} }} → {{sort | 1 = {{{sort value}}} | 2 = {{{displayed value}}} }} The first parameter is the sortkey; the second parameter is the wikitext of what has to be displayed. By default, this is the sortkey with link brackets: : {{Sort | 1 = {{{displayed value}}} }} → {{sort | 1 = {{{displayed value}}} }} A table using this template should use it in all rows. This template works by adding a hidden sort value, which replaces the value of the visible text for sorting purposes. The HTML output looks like: [[displayed value]]. =====Example===== |{{Sort|Brothers Karamazov|[[The Brothers Karamazov]]}} → |{{Sort|Brothers Karamazov|[[The Brothers Karamazov]]|demo=yes}} ;Sorting by numerical value, date, etc. :See [[Help:Sortable tables#Numerical sorting problems]] and [[meta:Help:Sorting#Sort modes]] ;Equal rank :If you simply code as the second parameter an indicator that two items are equally ranked, e.g. "4=", the template interpreter will treat this as an additional parameter (i.e. parameter 4, which it will then not use). You must explicitly code this as parameter 2: {{Sort|4|2=4=}} ==See also== * {{tiw|Dsv}} – a shortcut for data-sort-value="''foo''" * {{tiw|Sortname}} — for using names in sortable tables (using "last name, first name" as default sort key) * {{tiw|Date table sorting}} — for using dates in sortable tables * {{tiw|Number table sorting}} — for using alphabetic sort mode for numbers in sortable tables * [[Help:Sortable tables]] * [[meta:Help:Sorting]] {{Sandbox other|| [[Category:Sorting templates|{{PAGENAME}}]] }}

revision.comment: Bot: Adding dummy category to make categories work after wiki restoration
revision.text: {{documentation subpage}} {{Ombox|type=content|text=Instead of this template, consider using the data-sort-value attribute, like so: | data-sort-value="Doe, John" | John Doe. Please see {{Section link|WP:SORT|Specifying a sort key for a cell}} for details.}} {{#ifeq:{{SUBPAGENAME}}|sandbox||{{High-use}}}} {{lua|Module:Sort}} This template is for manually adjusting sorting order in sortable tables by specified sortkeys. ; Usage: : {{Sort | 1 = {{{sort value}}} | 2 = {{{displayed value}}} }} → {{sort | 1 = {{{sort value}}} | 2 = {{{displayed value}}} }} The first parameter is the sortkey; the second parameter is the wikitext of what has to be displayed. By default, this is the sortkey with link brackets: : {{Sort | 1 = {{{displayed value}}} }} → {{sort | 1 = {{{displayed value}}} }} A table using this template should use it in all rows. This template works by adding a hidden sort value, which replaces the value of the visible text for sorting purposes. The HTML output looks like: [[displayed value]]. =====Example===== |{{Sort|Brothers Karamazov|[[The Brothers Karamazov]]}} → |{{Sort|Brothers Karamazov|[[The Brothers Karamazov]]|demo=yes}} ;Sorting by numerical value, date, etc. :See [[Help:Sortable tables#Numerical sorting problems]] and [[meta:Help:Sorting#Sort modes]] ;Equal rank :If you simply code as the second parameter an indicator that two items are equally ranked, e.g. "4=", the template interpreter will treat this as an additional parameter (i.e. parameter 4, which it will then not use). You must explicitly code this as parameter 2: {{Sort|4|2=4=}} ==See also== * {{tiw|Dsv}} – a shortcut for data-sort-value="''foo''" * {{tiw|Sortname}} — for using names in sortable tables (using "last name, first name" as default sort key) * {{tiw|Date table sorting}} — for using dates in sortable tables * {{tiw|Number table sorting}} — for using alphabetic sort mode for numbers in sortable tables * [[Help:Sortable tables]] * [[meta:Help:Sorting]] {{Sandbox other|| [[Category:Sorting templates|{{PAGENAME}}]] }} [[Category:Templates]]

revision.comment: Bot: wrap 2 stray tag(s) in noinclude [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23606305292|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text: {{documentation subpage}} {{Ombox|type=content|text=Instead of this template, consider using the data-sort-value attribute, like so: | data-sort-value="Doe, John" | John Doe. Please see {{Section link|WP:SORT|Specifying a sort key for a cell}} for details.}} {{#ifeq:{{SUBPAGENAME}}|sandbox||{{High-use}}}} {{lua|Module:Sort}} This template is for manually adjusting sorting order in sortable tables by specified sortkeys. ; Usage: : {{Sort | 1 = {{{sort value}}} | 2 = {{{displayed value}}} }} → {{sort | 1 = {{{sort value}}} | 2 = {{{displayed value}}} }} The first parameter is the sortkey; the second parameter is the wikitext of what has to be displayed. By default, this is the sortkey with link brackets: : {{Sort | 1 = {{{displayed value}}} }} → {{sort | 1 = {{{displayed value}}} }} A table using this template should use it in all rows. This template works by adding a hidden sort value, which replaces the value of the visible text for sorting purposes. The HTML output looks like: [[displayed value]]. =====Example===== |{{Sort|Brothers Karamazov|[[The Brothers Karamazov]]}} → |{{Sort|Brothers Karamazov|[[The Brothers Karamazov]]|demo=yes}} ;Sorting by numerical value, date, etc. :See [[Help:Sortable tables#Numerical sorting problems]] and [[meta:Help:Sorting#Sort modes]] ;Equal rank :If you simply code as the second parameter an indicator that two items are equally ranked, e.g. "4=", the template interpreter will treat this as an additional parameter (i.e. parameter 4, which it will then not use). You must explicitly code this as parameter 2: {{Sort|4|2=4=}} ==See also== * {{tiw|Dsv}} – a shortcut for data-sort-value="''foo''" * {{tiw|Sortname}} — for using names in sortable tables (using "last name, first name" as default sort key) * {{tiw|Date table sorting}} — for using dates in sortable tables * {{tiw|Number table sorting}} — for using alphabetic sort mode for numbers in sortable tables * [[Help:Sortable tables]] * [[meta:Help:Sorting]] {{Sandbox other|| }} [[Category:Sorting templates|{{PAGENAME}}]] [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]]
